titleStmt.title: Telegram from the Managing Editor, Central News to the Secretary, Dublin Castle, 20 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Managing Editor, Central News

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a telegram from the managing editor of the London Central News Agency to the secretary in Dublin Castle appealing against a decision to publish exclusive reports on events concerning Dublin affairs through the Press Association instead of Central News.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO RP/1916/8640/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
XXX SECRETARY OF DUBLIN CASTLE DUBLIN OUR SPECIAL CORRESPONDENT IN DUBLIN MR FITZPATRICK STATES HE HAS BEEN
INFORMED THAT FURTHER OFFICIAL COMMUNIQUES RE DUBLIN AFFAIRS WILL BE MADE
EXCLUSIVELY THROUGH PRESS ASSOCIATION CORRESPONDENT WE PRESUME THERE HAS
BEEN SOME MISUNDERSTANDING THE CENTRAL NEWS IS THE LARGEST HOME AND
FOREIGN NEWS AGENCY IN THE WORLD IF YOU REQUIRE ANY INFORMATION AS TO OUR
(2) SECRETARY THE CASTLE DUBLIN
STANDING WE REFER YOU TO ANY OR ALL EDITORS OF DUBLIN NEWSPAPERS
MANAGING XXXXXXXXX EDITOR CENTRAL NEWS
